context.meal: chicken breast strips and hash brown

episode_text:
day=2; meal=chicken breast strips and hash brown; setting=Social with person on Right; time_of_day=morning;
preferences: microwave_time=2 min; occlusion_relevance=minimize right occlusion; robot_speed=medium; skewering_axis=parallel to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=yes; transfer_mode=outside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=medium; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=button; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=button; retract_between_bites=yes; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=1000 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a warm savory meal, THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user explicitly chose a longer reheat time for chicken and hash brown than in the quieter routine setting.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user explicitly chose to preserve visibility toward the right-side TV. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user again prioritized keeping the robot from blocking the right side, indicating sensitivity to right-side social/environmental engagement.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF feeding foods that require fork skewering AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any skewered food, THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the user has limited mouth opening and repeatedly selected the narrower-bite option compatible with that constraint.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is a skewered solid food meal, THEN yes BECAUSE the user explicitly chose confirmation in a social context, consistent with preferring extra verification when a failed bite transfer could be more awkward or noticeable.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a routine meal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user can lean forward comfortably and explicitly selected the less intrusive transfer style. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user maintained the same less intrusive transfer style in the social setting.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition in a routine meal, THEN near BECAUSE the user selected a close stopping distance while still preferring outside-mouth transfer. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN medium BECAUSE the user chose a slightly larger buffer in the social setting, suggesting more interpersonal comfort distance there.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a routine meal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN speech BECAUSE the user explicitly preferred spoken readiness cues. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE the user switched to a quieter visual cue in the social setting, indicating sensitivity to conversational or social disruption.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a routine meal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN button BECAUSE the user has reliable arm control, can press buttons easily, and limited mouth opening makes open-mouth readiness less suitable. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN button BECAUSE the user again preferred explicit manual signaling, consistent with favoring reliable control over open-mouth detection in social interaction contexts.

IF dining context is a routine meal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN button BECAUSE the user has reliable arm control and explicitly preferred direct manual signaling. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN button BECAUSE the user maintained explicit manual signaling in the social setting.

IF dining context is a routine meal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN button BECAUSE the user has reliable arm control and explicitly preferred direct manual signaling. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN button BECAUSE the user maintained explicit manual signaling in the social setting.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a routine meal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN speech BECAUSE the user explicitly preferred spoken completion cues. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE the user switched to a visual completion cue in the social setting, consistent with preferring less disruptive signaling around another person.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF feeding task AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN perception BECAUSE the user consistently selected the reliable automatic completion method for bites across observed contexts.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF drinking task AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN button BECAUSE the user consistently preferred explicit manual completion signaling rather than automatic completion.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF mouth wiping task AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN button BECAUSE the user consistently preferred explicit manual completion signaling rather than automatic completion.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN yes BECAUSE the user chose to reduce prolonged visual obstruction in a context where right-side visibility matters. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN yes BECAUSE the user again chose retraction when right-side engagement mattered, indicating willingness to accept longer meal time to reduce obstruction in socially relevant contexts.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food includes a dip or sauce AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is the observed TV-watching context, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user explicitly chose to avoid dipping despite sauce being available. IF food_condition is a meal without sauces or dips AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is social with a person on the right, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user selected no dipping in that meal as well.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a routine meal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user preferred a short timeout, consistent with favoring efficient progression when explicit button controls are available. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user chose a very long timeout in the social setting, indicating a strong preference to avoid unwanted automatic progression when attention may be shared with another person.
